archetypes.label: subway busker
barks: "Requests cost a dollar. Silence costs five."
"I played the last train out of Myrtle. Nobody clapped. They were busy crying."
"The tunnels sing back if you're honest with them."
"Tips fund the encore. The encore is me eating."

archetypes.label: scaffold rigger
barks: "That scaffold's held since '30. The building behind it, less so."
"The city wears more scaffold than skin at this point."
"Don't walk under anything I built in winter. I'll tell you which ones."

archetypes.label: drone spotter
barks: "That one's a courier bird. That one's somebody's eye. That one's mine."
"The sky's the only honest newspaper left."
"I log flight paths. People pay. I don't ask which people twice."

archetypes.label: relic dealer
barks: "Authenticated. By me. Just now."
"This candle burned through the dark years. Or a dark week. Memory is a market."
"Relics, friend. The past is the only thing still in production."
"Touch with your eyes. Buy with your grief."

archetypes.label: bike courier
barks: "Bridge toll went up. My rates follow physics."
"Don't ask what's in the bag. The bag pays better than you."
"Fastest thing left in this city is a bike and a grudge."

archetypes.label: ferry hand
barks: "Harbor's calm today. That's how it lies."
"We run when the tide says, not when the schedule says. The schedule drowned."
"Hold the rail. The rail held me through two surges."

archetypes.label: generator man
barks: "Power's two dollars an hour. Fumes included free."
"When the grid died I became a utility. Smallest one in history, best uptime."
"Don't touch the red one. The red one is why I have these gloves and this limp."

archetypes.label: bookmaker
barks: "Odds on anything. Weather, fights, ferry times, you making it home."
"The house wins. The house is a folding table, but it wins."
"Everybody pays Thursday. The ones who don't, you stop seeing Tuesdays."

archetypes.label: gleaner
barks: "The city throws nothing away. It just sets it down and forgets."
"Copper Tuesday, brass Friday. The week has a flavor if you work it."
"Everything I own was a building once."

archetypes.label: retired cop
barks: "Retired. The badge didn't get the memo and neither did the knees."
"Thirty years on the job, and the job left town before I did."
"I still walk the beat. Nobody pays me. So it's a calling or a condition."

archetypes.label: parkour kid
barks: "Your pockets ride lower than you think, old timer."
"Ground floor's for paying customers. I take the express."
"Catch me. Go ahead. The fire escape votes for me."

archetypes.label: fortune teller
barks: "The cards say flood. The cards have said flood since '31. The cards pay attention."
"Five dollars for the future. Ten if you want the good one."
"I told him yes, she still thinks of you. You sell what restocks."

archetypes.label: union steward
barks: "Scale wages or no wages. That's the whole sermon."
"The local's older than the grid and it'll outlive the grid. Already did once."
"You work, you're covered. You scab, you're cardio."

archetypes.label: night fisherman
barks: "The river gives at night. Daytime it just takes."
"The eels never left. We left the eels."
"Some nights the net comes up heavy and you pray it's fish."
